title: age-group — subprocessors
description: The age-group module engages ZERO third-party processors. Every field is stored in the tenant's own database. Age-group reference data feeds downstream Team + AthleteEnrollment modules; those modules engage their own subprocessors (Segment for analytics, S3 for cohort roster exports) — those are documented under their respective modules, not here.
notes: The default catalog reference table (`data/default-age-groups.json`) is a static shipped artifact. It contains NO tenant data — just the platform's baseline 10-entry catalog (U6..Masters). Consumers may cache it at the edge without concern.
Sport-cutoff samples (`data/sport-cutoff-samples.json`) are the same — canonical per-sport cutoff patterns from public federation documentation.

The AgeGroup row's downstream consumers may emit Athlete data (Wave 3+) to third-party subprocessors — but that data flow originates in the Athlete + AthleteEnrollment modules, not here. The age-group module never sees an Athlete ID.

If a future feature adds automated cutoff sync with sport federations (e.g. querying FIFA / FIBA / FINA age-group registries via API), that integration will engage a new subprocessor + this file will grow. Not in scope for Wave 3a.
